verbatim_text: And that sort of brings me to the end of the third part of the talk where we're looking at QDNano results. So moving on to my future work. Broadly, my goal is to make test time adaptation reliable, scalable, and a first class capability in modern AI systems. Now, at least I've seen some glimpses of this in theorem proving and research math. There's a lot to be done there, certainly a lot. But I would love to work with experts in areas like drug discovery and circuit design in order to be able to build practical systems and models that adapt at test time and solve challenging problems in these domains too. Now, to see how we can sort of get there, before that, I'd like to first characterize what kind of problems we're going to face roughly in these domains. The first problem is lack of data. There is literally no data. We have to go beyond frontiers of human knowledge in order to make discoveries happen in many of these domains. So that is a serious challenge. In addition to that, the solution space for some of these problems is exponentially large in some of the problem parameters. They're so big. So think about figuring out protein structures for drug discovery.
